digest.component_or_area: parallel-agent serial auto-lander (project infra, Gerrit + GitHub Actions)
propositions: Parallel agent sessions racing to land on the FFO main main cause redundant CI re-runs and manual rebase-retry overhead, burning wall-clock and agent attention.

A serial auto-lander service lands the front Autosubmit change/chain by rebasing to tip, awaiting a fresh Verified +1, and ancestor-atomic submitting the exact tested SHA, with zero further agent attention in the non-conflicting case.

The agent contract is one land command returning one typed terminal outcome (merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed) with the bot as single source of truth, so agents never correlate votes or Gerrit primitives.

The two-vote gate is never weakened: FFO backstop, strict Verified copyCondition, non-gating requester-votable Autosubmit label, and re-verify submittability on the exact tested SHA before submit (GerriScary posture).

The stack is the atomic unit for landing and failure — never partial-land or evict from within a stack; on failure the whole stack is handed back to its owning agent by removing Autosubmit and recording the outcome.

Cross-stack batching is rejected and deferred; landing logic stays platform-specific in project infra with no Gerrit-specific logic added to src/rebar core, and Phase-2 throughput (bounded speculation) is deferred to a separate idea.
